Turas Tabs
Interactive Crosstab Explorer
Turas Demo: Customer Experience Survey 2025
Prepared by The Research Lamppost for Demo
n = 1,000 19 Questions Weighted Created Apr 2026
i Reading the Dashboard tabs / dashboard_overview ▼
The dashboard summarises key metrics across all questions. Gauge cards show Total-column values with colour coding: green (strong), amber (moderate), red (low). The heatmap grid shows values across banner groups with the same colour logic. Hover any cell for the exact value. Use the metric type tabs to switch between percentages, means, and other metric types.
1,000
Total Respondents
Jan - Feb 2025
Fieldwork
19
Questions Analysed
4
Banner Groups
Region · Gender · Age · Customer Segment
Colour Key:Strong (NET≥+30 / Mean≥7.0 / Good or excellent≥60%)Moderate (NET≥+0 / Mean≥5.0 / Good or excellent≥40%)Concern (NET<+0 / Mean<5.0 / Good or excellent<40%)
i Reading the Dashboard tabs / dashboard_overview ▼
The dashboard summarises key metrics across all questions. Gauge cards show Total-column values with colour coding: green (strong), amber (moderate), red (low). The heatmap grid shows values across banner groups with the same colour logic. Hover any cell for the exact value. Use the metric type tabs to switch between percentages, means, and other metric types.

i Understanding Significance Tests tabs / significance_testing ▼
Significance letters (a, b, c…) indicate which column's value is statistically significantly higher than another at the chosen confidence level. A cell showing 'a' means that column's value is significantly higher than column a's value. No letter means no significant difference was detected. Significance depends on sample size — small sub-groups may show no differences even when values appear different.

NET POSITIVE (Promoter (9-10) - Detractor (0-6))7%23%▲CD25%▲CD-8%-30%6%7%12%-1%10%9%9%52%▲BCD7%-29%15%▲C
NPS Score7.023.025.0-8.0-30.06.07.012.0-1.010.09.09.052.07.0-29.015.0
✦ AI-assisted insight
The overall NPS score of 7 masks dramatic variation across regions and customer segments. Premium customers deliver a strong NPS of 52, significantly outperforming all other segments, while Budget customers show a deeply negative NPS of -29. Regionally, Gauteng (23) and Western Cape (25) perform well above the national average, but KwaZulu-Natal (-8) and particularly Eastern Cape (-30) show concerning negative scores. Gender and age groups show relatively flat patterns with no significant differences, suggesting the key drivers of satisfaction relate more to service tier and geographic delivery than demographic factors.

Mean6.26.06.46.46.16.16.36.16.26.16.76.05.66.56.55.9
✦ AI-assisted insight
Value for money ratings show a clear segment divide, with Premium customers scoring lowest at 5.6 mean rating compared to Standard (6.5) and Budget (6.5) segments, though no direct statistical comparison is flagged between these segments. The 45-54 age group stands out with the highest mean rating of 6.7, significantly above the 55+ group (6.0). Regional variations are modest, with Western Cape and KwaZulu-Natal both at 6.4 compared to Gauteng's 6.0, and Standard and Budget segments show significantly higher NET POSITIVE scores than Gauteng and Eastern Cape regions.

Mean6.67.37.06.45.06.86.56.26.86.76.76.76.96.56.76.6
✦ AI-assisted insight
Delivery experience shows stark regional disparities, with Eastern Cape performing dramatically worse than other provinces. Eastern Cape scores a mean of 5.0 compared to 7.3 in Gauteng and 7.0 in Western Cape, with only 23% rating delivery as good/excellent versus 66% in Gauteng and 61% in Western Cape. The Eastern Cape also shows the highest proportion of poor ratings at 24%, significantly above all other regions. Age and customer segment differences are minimal by comparison, suggesting the delivery challenge is primarily geographic rather than demographic.

Mean6.26.36.06.35.96.16.36.26.36.26.06.06.96.35.85.9
✦ AI-assisted insight
Mobile app experience shows a clear customer tier effect, with Premium customers rating significantly higher at 6.9 mean score and 55% net positive, compared to Budget customers at 5.8 mean score and 24% net positive. Regional variation is also evident, with Eastern Cape showing the lowest satisfaction at 5.9 mean score versus 6.3 in both Gauteng and KwaZulu-Natal. Premium customers demonstrate significantly higher satisfaction than Western Cape, KwaZulu-Natal, and Eastern Cape regional segments, while Budget and New Customer segments both struggle with only 24-25% achieving net positive ratings.
Slide Export

✦ AI-assisted insight
Communications quality ratings show stark regional variation, with Western Cape leading at 63% net positive compared to just 30% in Eastern Cape. The 33 percentage point gap between these regions is substantial and statistically significant. Premium customers achieve the highest satisfaction at 63% net positive, while other customer segments cluster between 44-52% with no significant differences between them.

✦ AI-assisted insight
Innovation perceptions show stark regional differences, with Gauteng and Western Cape customers significantly more positive than those in KwaZulu-Natal and Eastern Cape. Gauteng and Western Cape both achieve 60-61% positive ratings with net positive scores of 48% and 50% respectively, while KwaZulu-Natal drops to 42% positive with a net score of just 7%. Eastern Cape shows the most concerning pattern, with only 26% rating the company as innovative and a negative net score of -14%, indicating more customers view the company as not innovative than innovative in this region.

✦ AI-assisted insight
Sustainability commitment perceptions vary dramatically by customer segment, with Premium customers showing a NET POSITIVE score of 39% compared to Budget customers at -22%. The 55+ age group stands out with the highest NET POSITIVE score at 21%, while younger segments (18-24) show minimal positive sentiment at just 1%. Regional differences are more modest, though Eastern Cape shows a slightly negative NET POSITIVE score of -4% compared to other provinces which range from 7% to 10%.

✦ AI-assisted insight
Brand-values alignment shows a clear customer segment hierarchy, with Premium customers achieving a 52% net positive score compared to just 6% for Budget customers. The Premium segment significantly outperforms several regional segments including Western Cape (25%), KwaZulu-Natal (31%), and Eastern Cape (26%). Notably, the Budget segment shows the weakest alignment at 6% net positive, suggesting a potential disconnect between brand positioning and this customer group's values.

NET POSITIVE (Positive - Negative)11%33%▲CD30%▲CD-9%-32%9%13%11%3%15%15%16%7%13%9%12%
Index
Strongly disagree(1) = 1 to Strongly agree(5) = 5
3.23.53.52.92.53.13.23.23.13.23.33.23.13.23.13.2
✦ AI-assisted insight
After-sales support satisfaction shows stark regional disparities that demand attention. Eastern Cape delivers the poorest performance with 50% negative ratings and a net negative score of -32, while Gauteng and Western Cape perform significantly better with net positive scores of 33% and 30% respectively. KwaZulu-Natal also underperforms with 37% negative ratings and a net negative score of -9, creating a clear geographic divide in service quality. Gender, age, and customer segment show remarkably consistent satisfaction levels with no significant differences.

Significance testing: Column proportions z-test · with Bonferroni correction · p<0.05 · Minimum base n=30 · Generated by Turas Analytics

Added Slides

Open-ended findings, quotes, and narrative content. Double-click to edit, use markdown for formatting.

Formatting: **bold** · *italic* · ## Heading · - bullet · > quote
Methodology
Key Findings
+
Click 'Add Slide' to create content
Analyst Duncan Brett & Jess Taylor
Email duncan@researchlamppost.o.za ; jess@researchlamppost.co.za
Notes
AI methodology
AI-assisted insight callouts in this report are generated using claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Anthropic) and reviewed by the research team. They are clearly labelled in the report. AI callouts analyse data across all banner groups simultaneously and may reference subgroups not visible in the currently selected banner view. Strategic commentary and the executive summary are written by the research team.

Pinned Views

Pin questions from the Crosstabs tab to create a curated set of key findings.

📌
No pinned views yet.
Click the pin icon on any question in the Crosstabs tab to add it here.

Quick Guide

Everything you need to know to use this report

Navigating the Report

  • Sidebar Browse all questions. Type in the search box to filter.
  • Banner tabs Switch between cross-tabulation groups (e.g. Total, Age, Region).
  • Summary Dashboard with key metrics, gauges, and significant findings.
  • Crosstabs Full data tables with charts for every question.

Working with Tables

  • Column headers Click any header to sort the table by that column.
  • Column chips Toggle individual columns on or off to focus the view.
  • Heatmap Tick the Heatmap checkbox to colour-code cells by value.
  • Hide rows Tick to dim all data rows, then click individual rows to restore them.
  • Hide columns Tick to dim all columns, then use column chips to restore individual columns.
  • Show count Tick to display raw frequencies alongside percentages.

Charts

  • Chart toggle Tick the Chart checkbox to show or hide the chart.
  • Chart chips Select which columns appear in the chart to compare groups.
  • ✕ on rows Hover a data row and click ✕ to exclude it from the chart.

Adding Insights

  • + Add Insight Click below any question to add your analysis or commentary.
  • Summary text The Summary tab has editable text areas for Background and Executive Summary.

Pinning Key Findings

  • 📌 Pin Click the pin icon on any question to save it to your Pinned Views deck.
  • Pinned Views A curated set of your key findings. Drag to reorder, remove with ✕.
  • Section dividers Use 'Add Section' in Pinned Views to organise pins into groups.
  • Summary pins Pin gauge sections or sig findings from the Summary tab too.

Added Slides

  • Add Slide Create narrative slides with formatted text (supports **bold**, *italic*, bullets, headings).
  • 🖼 Add image Upload a chart, screenshot, or diagram to any slide. Images are resized automatically.
  • 📌 Pin slide Pin an Added Slide to include it alongside your data findings in Pinned Views.

Exporting & Sharing

  • Save Report Saves the report with all your insights, pins, and edits preserved. In Chrome/Edge a Save As dialog lets you choose the location and overwrite the original file. In other browsers the file downloads as <filename>_updated.html.
  • 📷 Export PNG Download any chart or pinned card as a high-resolution PNG image.
  • 📋 Copy Copy a chart or pin to your clipboard, then paste straight into PowerPoint.
  • Print / PDF Print your Pinned Views as a paginated document (one finding per page).
  • CSV / Excel Export table data for any question in spreadsheet format.
Tip: This report is a live working document. Add insights, pin key findings, create narrative slides, then Save Report to keep everything. In Chrome or Edge you can save directly over the original file. Re-open the saved file any time to continue where you left off. Press ? to show this guide again.
Click anywhere to close
